title: transform differential-conformance fixture file
description: Describes a set of JSONata transform cases. Two kinds share this file shape: `agree` cases (the cross-SDK gate) pin the NORMATIVE jsonata-js output that every conformant engine MUST reproduce; `known-divergence` cases catalog, per root-cause pattern, where the implementation engine deviates from that normative output. An outcome is a small envelope so `undefined` stays distinct from a `null` value and error identity is a message substring rather than exact text.

expectedEngine.description: The engine that produced every `expected` outcome — the normative tiebreak the spec pins (jsonata-js@2.1.1).

actualEngine.description: known-divergence only: the engine that produced every `actual` outcome (e.g. gnata@0.2.2).

rootCauses.description: known-divergence only: prose for each root-cause label (A-F).

outcome.description: The result of evaluating `expr` against `input`. `value` carries any JSON value; `status: undefined` is JSONata's no-result signal (distinct from a null value); `status: error` records only a message substring, since exact error text is engine-specific. `orderNondeterministic` (an `actual` only) marks a value whose array-element ORDER varies per process — compared as a multiset, never positionally.

rootCause.description: known-divergence only: which root-cause pattern this case exercises.

input.description: The JSON value the expression evaluates against (JSONata's context, $).
